req.title: Rate limiting (negative variant)
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-074 mandates a 60 req/min per-user rate limit with 429+Retry-After on overage. Three test cases are designed: (1) a positive EP case confirming requests within quota succeed (2xx); (2) a negative NEG/DT case confirming the 61st request returns 429 with a Retry-After header; (3) a boundary BVA case probing the exact 60th request (last allowed) returns non-429 and the 61st triggers enforcement. Each test is self-contained via a unique per-test user token fixture, ensuring idempotency. State resets are achieved by using fresh authenticated identities so prior window state cannot bleed across runs.

tests.name: test_requests_within_quota_succeed
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A valid user auth token is available via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
The rate-limit window for the token is clean (new identity)
actions: Send 10 GET requests to /accounts using the fresh token
Collect HTTP status codes for all responses
expected: All 10 responses return HTTP 200 or another 2xx success code
trace: R-FIN-074: requests below 60/min threshold must not be throttled
EP partition: request count in [1, 60] -> success class

tests.name: test_sixty_first_request_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A valid user auth token is available via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
The rate-limit window for the token is clean (new identity)
actions: Send 60 GET requests to /accounts using the fresh token to exhaust the quota
Send a 61st GET request to /accounts with the same token
Inspect the HTTP status code and headers of the 61st response
expected: The 61st response returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
The 61st response includes a Retry-After header with a positive integer value
trace: R-FIN-074: overages shall return 429 with retry-after
R-FIN-074: exceeded limits negative path must be tested
NEG: deliberate overage to verify enforcement and error payload

tests.name: test_boundary_60th_allowed_and_61st_rejected
preconditions: Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
A valid user auth token is available via <fixture: fresh_user_token>
The rate-limit window for the token is clean (new identity)
actions: Send 59 GET requests to /accounts to reach one below the boundary
Send the 60th GET request (exact boundary) and record its status
Send the 61st GET request (boundary+1) and record its status and Retry-After header
expected: The 60th request (boundary) returns a non-429 status code (2xx)
The 61st response Retry-After header is present and its value is a positive integer <= 60
trace: R-FIN-074: 60 requests per minute per user - boundary at exactly 60
BVA: on-boundary (60th) must succeed; off-boundary (61st) must fail
R-FIN-074: retry-after value should reflect remaining window seconds

{
  "requirement_id": "R-FIN-074",
  "reasoning": "R-FIN-074 mandates a 60 req/min per-user rate limit with 429+Retry-After on overage. Three test cases are designed: (1) a positive EP case confirming requests within quota succeed (2xx); (2) a negative NEG/DT case confirming the 61st request returns 429 with a Retry-After header; (3) a boundary BVA case probing the exact 60th request (last allowed) returns non-429 and the 61st triggers enforcement. Each test is self-contained via a unique per-test user token fixture, ensuring idempotency. State resets are achieved by using fresh authenticated identities so prior window state cannot bleed across runs.",
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_requests_within_quota_succeed",
      "kind": "positive",
      "heuristic": "EP",
      "preconditions": [
        "Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL",
        "A valid user auth token is available via <fixture: fresh_user_token>",
        "The rate-limit window for the token is clean (new identity)"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Send 10 GET requests to /accounts using the fresh token",
        "Collect HTTP status codes for all responses"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "All 10 responses return HTTP 200 or another 2xx success code",
        "No response returns HTTP 429"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-074: requests below 60/min threshold must not be throttled",
        "EP partition: request count in [1, 60] -> success class"
      ],
      "executable": "import pytest\nimport requests\n\nBASE_URL = \"https://banking-api.example.com\"\nREQUEST_COUNT = 10\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef fresh_user_token():\n    return <fixture: fresh_user_token>\n\ndef test_requests_within_quota_succeed(fresh_user_token):\n    headers = {\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {fresh_user_token}\"}\n    statuses = []\n    for _ in range(REQUEST_COUNT):\n        r = requests.get(f\"{BASE_URL}/accounts\", headers=headers, timeout=10)\n        statuses.append(r.status_code)\n    assert all(s < 400 for s in statuses), (\n        f\"Expected all 2xx but got: {statuses}\"\n    )\n    assert 429 not in statuses, \"Rate limit triggered within quota\""
    },
    {
      "name": "test_sixty_first_request_returns_429_with_retry_after",
      "kind": "negative",
      "heuristic": "NEG",
      "preconditions": [
        "Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL",
        "A valid user auth token is available via <fixture: fresh_user_token>",
        "The rate-limit window for the token is clean (new identity)"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Send 60 GET requests to /accounts using the fresh token to exhaust the quota",
        "Send a 61st GET request to /accounts with the same token",
        "Inspect the HTTP status code and headers of the 61st response"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "The 61st response returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests",
        "The 61st response includes a Retry-After header with a positive integer value"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-FIN-074: overages shall return 429 with retry-after",
        "R-FIN-074: exceeded limits negative path must be tested",
        "NEG: deliberate overage to verify enforcement and error payload"
      ],
      "executable": "import pytest\nimport requests\n\nBASE_URL = \"https://banking-api.example.com\"\nQUOTA = 60\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef fresh_user_token():\n    return <fixture: fresh_user_token>\n\ndef test_sixty_first_request_returns_429_with_retry_after(fresh_user_token):\n    headers = {\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {fresh_user_token}\"}\n    for _ in range(QUOTA):\n        requests.get(f\"{BASE_URL}/accounts\", headers=headers, timeout=10)\n    overage = requests.get(f\"{BASE_URL}/accounts\", headers=headers, timeout=10)\n    assert overage.status_code == 429, (\n        f\"Expected 429, got {overage.status_code}\"\n    )\n    retry_after = overage.headers.get(\"Retry-After\")\n    assert retry_after is not None, \"Retry-After header missing on 429\"\n    assert int(retry_after) >
